figures.caption: TABLE III: Performance comparison of EMSpice 3 across benchmark designs. A TTF entry of > 5.0 × 107 s indicates that the 10% IR-drop failure threshold was not reached within the simulated time window. The Krylov metric error is measured on TTF when failure occurs, and on the final IR drop otherwise.
Fig. 10: Temperature maps, stress distributions, and IR drop maps for the ARM Cortex-A logic core under two thermal scenarios

Fig. 5: Steady-state stress distribution for the RISC-V core (Case 1, baseline external temperature map at 353 K average) computed without void nucleation. Stress accumulates unbounded throughout the simulation, revealing the segments most susceptible to EM degradation prior to any saturation effect.
Fig. 9: Power grid structure of the ARM Cortex-A logic core (three metal layers M1, M8, M9 shown together with VIA connections)
